video.title: Giro d'Italia 2020 - Stage 6 Highlights | Cycling | Eurosport

transcript.clean_text: clear skies and sunshine along with beautiful landscapes breeded day six of the judo italia a day beginning in castro villarreal and ending in matera 188 kilometers breakaway or sprint that again would be the question a headwind in the final 50 kilometers perhaps making things a little bit more difficult for any attackers just one king of the mountains two sprints the action ready to get on the way four riders who get up the road not as much of a fight as expected to get in that break three second division italian teams represented by by zana and fraporti with wieland there from the world tour they get nine and a half minutes as a maximum gap at the thought borah hansgroh decided to go to work at the first intermediate sprints it would be an opportunity for androni jocatly to take maximum points mattia baies taking the 12 points behind peter sagan wanting to try and keep the malaya chiclamino however arnold de mar being the quickest on they'd go and borrow handscrew would continue to work once they seriously set about their job the gap would tumble down the headwinds starting to come into effect inside the final 50 kilometers after the second final intermediate sprint bonus seconds available here marco frapporti taking them but already 52 minutes behind not really doing much for his challenge fraporti and vini zabu more about the breakaways and stage hunting looking for a first win in eight years on the judo it wouldn't come on this day though hiccup for the malayals on the way in but the gap by this point with 35 k's left was already down to two minutes joao almeida would be back in the bunch and on the way to the only king of the mountains point of the day james whelan would show he was the stronger of the four riders australian rider from melbourne 24 years of age and only in his second year as a pro former winner of the under-23 rendezvous showing his strength thomas again would try over the top he'd at least pick up points in the king of the mountains classification but the blue jersey staying on the back of people pipagana for another day whelan caught with 13 and a half k's to go and a tricky run into matera meaning that it would not be easy to the line several sprinters dropped and even most of the gc guys to the four matteo fabric was trying to make it as hard as possible so that pitter segan could win a sprint arnold de mar losing places inside the final two k's regaining those positions with 300 meters to go following aston into the line and launching a deadly sprint with 200 meters remaining an imperious win for the french champion having to work really hard inside the final couple of kilometers and getting a wonderful reward them are so explosive and the best by a long long way to the line matthew's up there felina too with molano and vendrame winning the stage for a second time in six days and taking them in the process segan only able to finish down in eighth place and now on the podium for a second time this time in the beautiful city of matera in the santa fe kept the pink jersey whilst arnold imar swapped his french national champions jersey for that the points jersey 43 seconds the lead for the portuguese almeida coming up on stage you
